title: Cheeyappara Waterfalls
tagline: Cheeyappara Waterfalls — heritage city of Kerala

overview.short: The Cheeyappara Waterfalls is a waterfall in the Idukki district, Kerala, India.
overview.description: The Cheeyappara Waterfalls is a waterfall in the Idukki district, Kerala, India. It is located alongside the National Highway 85, between Neriamangalam and Adimali. The Cheeyappara Waterfalls cascades down in seven steps. It is also reachable via trekking and hiking trails.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Pazhampillichal
topPlaces.description: Pazhampillichal is a village in the High Range district Idukki of Kerala State, India. It is one of the far-flung forest fringe villages in the Munnar/Devikulam Wildlife, deciduous and evergreen forest, connecting from Aarammile (6th Mile) which is in between Adimaly - Neriamangalam on the Munnar – Cochin State Highway.

topPlaces.name: Neriamangalam Bridge
topPlaces.description: The Neriamangalam Bridge is a bridge in the South Indian state of Kerala, that connects the Ernakulam and Idukki districts. Opened in 1935, it is the first Class A arch bridge in Asia.

topPlaces.name: Meenuliyan Para
topPlaces.description: Meenuliyan Para is a prominent mountain peak located near Thodupuzha in the Idukki district of Kerala, India. Characterized by a massive rock formation that rises over 4000 feet, the mountain is named for the fish-scale appearance of its rocky surface.

topPlaces.name: Vannappuram
topPlaces.description: Vannappuram is a village in the Idukki district of state Kerala in India . The village is in the highland regions and is loacated 17 km north of Thodupuzha Town and 28 km east of Muvattupuzha Town and lies north of the Kaliyar River.

topPlaces.name: Kattadikkadavu
topPlaces.description: Kattadikkadavu is a hillock and trekking spot located in Vannapuram panchayat near Thodupuzha in Idukki district, Kerala, India. It is also located 34 km from Muvattupuzha in Ernakulam district.

topPlaces.name: Mannamkandam
topPlaces.description: Mannamkandam is a village in Devikulam Taluk, Idukki District, Kerala, India. It is located near the town of Adimali, approximately 22 kilometres northwest of the district capital Painavu, and 26 kilometres west of the taluk capital Devikulam.

topPlaces.name: Kaliyar
topPlaces.description: Kaliyar is a small village in the Idukki district, Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Neriamangalam
topPlaces.description: Neriamangalam is a village in Ernakulam district in the Indian state of Kerala. Neriamangalam is on the banks of Periyar River.

Kaliyar is a small village in the Idukki district, Kerala, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Cheeyappara Waterfalls?
faq.a: The best time to visit Cheeyappara Waterfalls is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Cheeyappara Waterfalls?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (48 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (60 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Cheeyappara Waterfalls?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Cheeyappara Waterfalls at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Cheeyappara Waterfalls?
faq.a: Stays in Cheeyappara Waterfalls start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Cheeyappara Waterfalls?
faq.a: The top attractions are Pazhampillichal, Neriamangalam Bridge, Meenuliyan Para, Vannappuram, Kattadikkadavu.
